sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Player moved LEFT. Now let me check the player position and try clicking on all green blocks from left to right, top to bottom. The green blocks are at rows 25 and 31 (two rows of 7 blocks each).
Let me first check the exact positions of all green blocks.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check player position and green block positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
player = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'b']
if player:
    p = player[0]
    b = p['boundary']
    min_c = min(pt[1] for pt in b)
    print(f"Player col: {min_c}")

green = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"Green blocks: {len(green)}")
for g in sorted(green, key=lambda n: (min(pt[0] for pt in n['boundary']), min(pt[1] for pt in n['boundary']))):
    b = g['boundary']
    min_r = min(pt[0] for pt in b)
    min_c = min(pt[1] for pt in b)
    print(f"  Green at ({min_r}, {min_c})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Player is now at col 15 (left side). Green blocks are at:
- Row 25: cols 13, 19, 25, 31, 37, 43, 49 (7 blocks)
- Row 31: cols 13, 19, 25, 31, 37, 43, 49 (7 blocks)
Now let me try clicking on all 14 green blocks, starting from the top-left and going row by row. The center of each green block would be approximately at (row+2, col+2) since they're 4x4 blocks. But actually, looking at the segmentation, the boundary gives the corners. Let me use the center of the block.
For a 4x4 block at (25, 13), the center would be around (27, 15). Let me click on the center of each block.
Actually, looking at the previous successful clicks in Level 1, I clicked at positions like (3, 15), (3, 21), (3, 27), etc. These are the centers of the green blocks.
For the current green blocks:
- Row 25, col 13: center at (27, 15)
- Row 25, col 19: center at (27, 21)
- Row 25, col 25: center at (27, 27)
- Row 25, col 31: center at (27, 33)

- Row 31, col 37: center at (33, 39)
- Row 31, col 43: center at (33, 45)
- Row 31, col 49: center at (33, 51)
Let me try clicking on all 14 green blocks in order. But wait, in previous attempts, clicking on ~10 green blocks caused the board to transform. Maybe the order matters?
In Level 1, I clicked on green blocks in this order:
- (3, 15), (3, 21), (3, 27) - top row
- (15, 33), (15, 39), (15, 45), (15, 51) - bottom row
So I clicked on the top row first (left to right), then the bottom row (left to right). But the bottom row started at col 33, not col 15. This suggests the green blocks in Level 1 were at different positions.
Let me try the same order for Level 2: top row first (left to right), then bottom row (left to right).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click on all 14 green blocks in order: top row left to right, then bottom row left to right
# Top row (row 25): centers at (27, 15), (27, 21), (27, 27), (27, 33), (27, 39), (27, 45), (27, 51)
# Bottom row (row 31): centers at (33, 15), (33, 21), (33, 27), (33, 33), (33, 39), (33, 45), (33, 51)

]

action(clicks)
